This article applies to selling in: **United States**

# Seller Challenge FAQ

Sign in to use the tool and get personalized help (desktop browser required).

[](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/sign-in?use_default_idp&redirect_to=%2Fhelp%2Fhub%2Freference%2Fexternal%2FG7XWXBNSYMBJAPX5)

[](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/help/hub/reference/external/G7XWXBNSYMBJAPX5)

[](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/help/hub/reference/external/G7XWXBNSYMBJAPX5)

**Note:**Currently Seller Challenge is available only to US-based sellers enrolled in the Account Health Assurance program.

#### What is the Seller Challenge benefit?

#### What is the Seller Challenge benefit?

The Seller Challenge gives Account Health Assurance (AHA) sellers the ability to request an enhanced review of specific enforcements or decisions after multiple attempts to resolve them. AHA sellers receive:

*   Three (3) Seller Challenges initially
*   Each failed challenge replenishes after six (6) months

#### How do I use a Seller Challenge?

1.   Go to the Product Policy Compliance page
2.   Start a response to an issue
3.   Look for the Seller Challenge text on eligible enforcements
4.   Select the Seller Challenge to access the submission form

**Note:**We aim to respond to all cases with a decision within 48 hours.

#### What happens to my unused Seller Challenges if I lose AHA eligibility?

If you lose AHA eligibility:

*   You will no longer have access to your remaining Seller Challenges
*   Unused Seller Challenges will only become available when you regain AHA eligibility

#### Can I use my Seller Challenge on any enforcement?

#### Can I use my Seller Challenge on any enforcement?

You can use Seller Challenges on eligible listing-level enforcements only. Eligible enforcements are identified by:

*   A Seller Challenge icon
*   Seller Challenge text

#### Can I have multiple challenges open at the same time?

Yes, you can have multiple open challenges simultaneously. However, you can only use one Seller Challenge per enforcement.

#### What happens to my listing while a challenge is being reviewed?

If your listing is deactivated when you start the Seller Challenge, it will remain deactivated following standard Amazon policies while the challenge is in review.

#### If I run out of Seller Challenges, will I still have an escalation path for my issues?

Yes. Even without a Seller Challenge, or if you don't have any eligible issues to apply a Seller Challenge to, you will continue to have access to all other existing remediation paths on listing issues.

This article applies to selling in: **United States**

# Seller Challenge FAQ

Sign in to use the tool and get personalized help (desktop browser required).

[](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/sign-in?use_default_idp&redirect_to=%2Fhelp%2Fhub%2Freference%2Fexternal%2FG7XWXBNSYMBJAPX5)

[](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/help/hub/reference/external/G7XWXBNSYMBJAPX5)

[](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/help/hub/reference/external/G7XWXBNSYMBJAPX5)

**Note:**Currently Seller Challenge is available only to US-based sellers enrolled in the Account Health Assurance program.

#### What is the Seller Challenge benefit?

#### What is the Seller Challenge benefit?

The Seller Challenge gives Account Health Assurance (AHA) sellers the ability to request an enhanced review of specific enforcements or decisions after multiple attempts to resolve them. AHA sellers receive:

*   Three (3) Seller Challenges initially
*   Each failed challenge replenishes after six (6) months

#### How do I use a Seller Challenge?

1.   Go to the Product Policy Compliance page
2.   Start a response to an issue
3.   Look for the Seller Challenge text on eligible enforcements
4.   Select the Seller Challenge to access the submission form

**Note:**We aim to respond to all cases with a decision within 48 hours.

#### What happens to my unused Seller Challenges if I lose AHA eligibility?

If you lose AHA eligibility:

*   You will no longer have access to your remaining Seller Challenges
*   Unused Seller Challenges will only become available when you regain AHA eligibility

#### Can I use my Seller Challenge on any enforcement?

#### Can I use my Seller Challenge on any enforcement?

You can use Seller Challenges on eligible listing-level enforcements only. Eligible enforcements are identified by:

*   A Seller Challenge icon
*   Seller Challenge text

#### Can I have multiple challenges open at the same time?

Yes, you can have multiple open challenges simultaneously. However, you can only use one Seller Challenge per enforcement.

#### What happens to my listing while a challenge is being reviewed?

If your listing is deactivated when you start the Seller Challenge, it will remain deactivated following standard Amazon policies while the challenge is in review.

#### If I run out of Seller Challenges, will I still have an escalation path for my issues?

Yes. Even without a Seller Challenge, or if you don't have any eligible issues to apply a Seller Challenge to, you will continue to have access to all other existing remediation paths on listing issues.
